title: 65-lesson checkpoint: sustained speed
description: Mid-Block 5 speed checkpoint — full paragraph, no breaks
text: Two-thirds through the course now. Your fingers know the keyboard better than you think. Trust them. Read ahead by a word or two, breathe steadily, and let the text flow from your hands without thinking about which finger goes where.

tips.text: Two-thirds of the way through. Long enough to see real change in your hands, far enough to know you will finish.
Keep an eye on your best speed. If the number is still rising, however slowly, you are on the right road. A little every day does this.

tips.target: Two-thirds through the course now. Your fingers know the keyboard better than you think. Trust them. Read ahead by a

tips.text: If you stall, ease off accuracy for a run or two and let the speed reach further. You can tighten the aim again once the hands remember the faster pace.

tips.target: word or two, breathe steadily, and let the text flow from your hands without thinking about which finger goes where.

character_tips.anna: Two-thirds done. By now your fingers know more than your conscious mind does. Trust them and look ahead. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.maxim: A good stretch of lessons behind you. Check your number; it should be sitting higher than when you began. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.knopych: Past the halfway mark, closer to the end than the start. Keep the steady pace that got you here. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.klavochka: Nearly there, and the rhythm is yours now. Hold it, and the rest falls into place. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
